name: Jadraque Borderlands Loop
name_original: Castilblanco de Henares desde Jadraque — circular por El Camino del Cid
description: Cycle a moderate 15.3-mile (24.6 km) circular route from Jadraque to Castilblanco de Henares, exploring El Camino del Cid's rural beauty.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Jadraque offers routes through varied landscapes, including historical paths like the Camino del Cid and scenic loops through valleys and past castles. The region features a mix of open borderlands and areas around the Alcorlo Reservoir. Elevation changes are present, with some routes featuring significant climbs. Best touring cycling routes around Jadraque The most popular touring cycling route is Mandayona and Ledanca from Jadraque — circular by the Camino del Cid, a 37.3 miles (60.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This route follows a circular path along the historic Camino del Cid. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Jadraque Borderlands Loop, a moderate 15.3 miles (24.6 km) path. This loop explores the varied terrain of the Jadraque borderlands. Local touring cyclists also love the Jadraque y Hita desde Carrascosa de Henares — circular a los Valles y Castillos, a 35.9 miles (57.8 km) trail leading through valleys and past castles, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Jadraque is defined by historical routes, open borderlands, and scenic valleys. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Jadraque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for touring cycling routes around Jadraque?
A: The touring cycling routes in Jadraque primarily cater to more experienced cyclists. Out of the 10 routes available, 9 are classified as difficult, and 1 is moderate. There are no easy routes listed in this guide.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes in Jadraque?
A: Yes, there is one moderate touring cycling route available: the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188060077/jadraque-borderlands-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque Borderlands Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 15.3 miles (24.6 km) long and explores the varied terrain of the region.

Q: What is the typical length of touring cycling routes in the Jadraque area?
A: The routes vary in length, with the longest being approximately 37.3 miles (60.0 km) and the shortest moderate route at 15.3 miles (24.6 km). Most difficult routes are in the range of 35-37 miles (56-60 km).

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on a touring bike ride near Jadraque?
A: You can expect a diverse landscape featuring historical paths like the Camino del Cid, scenic loops through valleys, and routes passing by impressive castles. The region also includes open borderlands and areas around the Alcorlo Reservoir.

Q: What historical sites or natural landmarks can I see while cycling in Jadraque?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185667315/jadraque-y-hita-desde-carrascosa-de-henares-circular-a-los-valles-y-castillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque y Hita desde Carrascosa de Henares — circular a los Valles y Castillos&lt;/a&gt; route leads through valleys and past castles. You can also explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hita&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6594742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan Bautista of Jadraque&lt;/a&gt;. Natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares River Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcorlo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185715592/borderlands-and-alcorlo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borderlands and Alcorlo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Jadraque?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Jadraque are circular. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188076444/mandayona-and-ledanca-from-jadraque-circular-by-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mandayona and Ledanca from Jadraque — circular by the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188060077/jadraque-borderlands-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque Borderlands Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185715592/borderlands-and-alcorlo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borderlands and Alcorlo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Jadraque?
A: Given the region's climate, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling in Jadraque, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there any routes that follow historical paths?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188076444/mandayona-and-ledanca-from-jadraque-circular-by-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mandayona and Ledanca from Jadraque — circular by the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt; route specifically follows a section of the historic Camino del Cid, offering a journey through a significant cultural landscape.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Jadraque?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the varied terrain, the historical significance of routes like the Camino del Cid, and the scenic beauty of the valleys and castles. The region offers a good challenge for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Is there a route that includes the San Blas Church?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jadraque – San Blas Church loop from Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that includes the San Blas Church as a point of interest.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Jadraque guide?
A: This guide features a total of 10 touring cycling routes around Jadraque, offering various options for exploring the region.
tips: Castilblanco de Henares is a small rural town on the banks of the Cañamares River before it flows into the Henares River. You can rest in the Plaza Mayor or in the Church of Nuestra Señora del Rosario.

Hard to miss in Jadraque: the fortress high up on the castle hill, also called "Castillo del Cid". Its current appearance can be traced back to the 15th century, but it was probably already there as a somewhat smaller fortress during the times of the caliphate.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route begins and ends in Jadraque. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular tour.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: This route, part of the broader El Camino del Cid, primarily uses quiet minor roads and well-maintained rural tracks. It's suitable for touring, hybrid, and even road bikes, indicating generally good surfaces. While the overall Camino del Cid can have hilly sections, this particular route is classified as moderate with no major technical challenges.

faqs.question: What can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of historical and natural sights. You'll pass through the town of Jadraque, known for its castle. In Castilblanco de Henares, you can see the Church of Our Lady of the Rosary and a leafy poplar grove by the Cañamares river, a pleasant spot for a break. The landscape features broad plains, fertile fields, and lush riverside forests along the Cañamares and Henares rivers.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Classified as a 'moderate' touring bicycle route, it is generally suitable for cyclists with some experience. The surfaces are good, and it avoids major technical challenges, making it accessible for those comfortable with a 24.6 km ride and around 293 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: Is the Castilblanco de Henares from Jadraque — circular by El Camino del Cid route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows public roads and tracks, which are generally accessible, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near agricultural areas. Ensure your dog is comfortable with cycling speeds and the distance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences varied seasons. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are generally ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and often vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so consider early morning rides if cycling during these months. Winters are cooler but often still cyclable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this section of El Camino del Cid. It uses public roads and tracks.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route overlaps with several other official trails. You'll find sections that are part of the Tierras de Frontera, Camino del Cid (Senderista), and GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, among others. The entire Camino del Cid is generally well-signposted.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Jadraque, as the starting and ending point, offers amenities such as cafes and restaurants. Castilblanco de Henares is a smaller village, but you might find basic services. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially during off-peak seasons, as services in smaller villages can be limited.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate touring bicycle route, essential items include a well-maintained touring or hybrid bicycle, a helmet, sufficient water, snacks, basic repair kit (spare tube, pump), sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses), and appropriate clothing for the weather. A phone with GPS or a map is also recommended, though the route is well-signposted.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 24.6 km touring bicycle route is approximately 1 hour and 40 minutes of active cycling time. This can vary based on your pace, stops for sightseeing, and fitness level.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Rosary, Castilblanco de Henares
